figures.caption: Figure 2: Pareto frontier on LongMemEval-S Full-500: input tokens per query (log scale) vs official binary-judge accuracy. ZenBrain (red star) dominates the other k=5 retrieval baselines (gray circles); the long-context oracle (blue diamond) achieves only 4.5 pp higher absolute accuracy at ∼106× the token budget. Error bar on ZenBrain is the bootstrap 95% CI across seeds {42, 123, 456} (too small to be visually distinguishable from the marker at this scale). The dotted line connects ZenBrain and the oracle on the Pareto frontier.

Table 7: Full ablation study under moderate conditions (300 facts, 100 queries, 45-day aging, decay=0.15). Quality = retention × P@5. ∆Q shows relative change vs. full system. Mean over 10 seeds. ∗p < 0.005 (Wilcoxon).

Table 8: Ablation under challenging conditions (400 facts, 50-day aging, decay=0.20). Seven algorithms become individually significant, revealing the gradient between cooperative redundancy and individual criticality. Mean over 10 seeds. ∗p < 0.005 (Wilcoxon).

Figure 3: Retention curves over 30 days (10 runs, 1,000 facts each). Pure Ebbinghaus decays to 0% by day 7. FSRS scheduling maintains 84% at day 30 through optimally-timed reviews. ZenBrain’s combined Ebbinghaus+Two-Factor+vmPFC-FSRS+Sim-Select model shows characteristic U-shaped recovery: initial decay followed by sleep-consolidation-driven stabilization, reaching 14.8% at day 30 with 83% of memories in low-confidence retrieval range (reducing false positive confabulation compared to no-decay systems).

Table 13: Routing ablation study. Disabled layers reroute content to the next available layer. ∆F1 shows relative change vs. full system. Mean ± std over 10 seeds. ∗p < 0.005 (Wilcoxon signed-rank; threshold is more stringent than the Bonferroni-corrected α = 0.05/8 = 0.00625 for K = 8 ablation contrasts).
Table 1: Comparison of memory systems for LLM agents. ✓ = supported, ✗ = absent.
Table 18: Key hyperparameters and their values.

Table 3: LongMemEval-S Full-500 cross-benchmark replication under a unified nomic-embed-text (768-dim) embedding backbone for all four systems. Per-question isolation: reset() → ingest(∼494 turns) → query(k=5). Retrieval metrics (P@5, R@5, MRR, NDCG@5, F1) are averaged over each system’s successfully-completed queries (zenbrain/a-mem: n=500; mem0: n=496; letta: n=441, see †). Judge columns are LLM-as-Judge normalized means over all 500 queries (letta’s 59 InternalServerError cases and mem0’s 4 embedder-400 cases appear as empty retrievals and are scored by the judge as 0/5). zenbrain uses three retrieval seeds (42, 123, 456) with identical deterministic outputs; a-mem, mem0, and letta use seed=42. Bold = best per column; intersect-subgroup numbers on the n=441 tasks all four systems serve are discussed in the text (§6.3). Under Bonferroni correction (α=0.05/18=2.78×10−3, 18 primary tests = 6 pair-wise comparisons × 3 judges), all three ZenBrain-vs-competitor gaps clear significance on all three judges (min p=6.20×10−31, max p=2.81×10−6; full table in Appendix P).
Table 23: Per-category F1 on MemoryArena. Cross-session inference requires combining information from 2+ earlier sessions. Best per column in bold. Mean over 10 seeds.
Figure 1: ZenBrain architecture. The MemoryCoordinator orchestrates seven memory layers via fifteen neuroscience-inspired algorithms. Arrows indicate store/recall/consolidate/decay operations.

Table 9: Stress ablation study (500 facts, 150 queries, 60-day aging, decay=0.25). Under extreme pressure, 9 of 15 algorithms show significant individual contributions. Mean over 10 seeds. ∗p < 0.005 (Wilcoxon).

Table 4: Algorithm criticality gradient across three difficulty levels. ∆Q (%) relative to full system at each level. ∗p < 0.005 (Wilcoxon, 10 seeds). Algorithms sorted by challenging-condition impact. Per-level tables, integration cascade, and effect-size breakdowns: Appendix G.

Table 15: Two-Factor KG dynamics and Bayesian confidence propagation. Top: retrieval P@5 with importance-weighted vs uniform edges. Bottom: pairwise AUC before/after propagation. Mean ± std over 10 seeds.
